def: n. m. S. झाट a wood. 1. A shrub; bramble; brushwood; thorny bush es; brake. 2. A lustre or chandelier. 3. A kind of firework. jhāṛ bāṅdhnā , v. n. To pour down in torrents; to shower; pour. Gāliyoṅ kā jhāṛ bāṅdh dīyā. jhāṛ bisānā , v. a. To pick a quarrel. jhāṛ jhaṅkkār , n. m. A tangled thicket; a brake; underwood. jhāṛ sī , ( Bot .) Cymose. jhāṛ kā kāṅṭā , n. m. 1. Thorns of brambles. 2. A quarrelsome person. Jhāṛ ke kāṅṭe se kaun uljhe? Wom. jhāṛ-khanḍ , n. m. A forest; jungle. jhāṛ ho-kar lipaṭnā, jhāṛ kā kāṅṭā h., jhāṛ h . v. n. 1. To cling to or catch as a bramble; to tangle. 2. To stick like a leech. Na jhāṛā gair ko hargiz, ki ho-kar jhāṛ lipṭā thā , Zauq. Mujhī par gālioṅ kā jhāṛ tū ne bad-zabāṅ bāṅdhā . You never shook them off when others stuck like thorns to you, On me alone, foul-mouthed, you shower abuses not a few. kauṛiyoṅ kā jhāṛ , ( Slang .) n. m. 1. A toy. 2. Fac. Membrum virile .
E. n. f. A storm of wind and rain.
s.f. Sweeping, cleaning, &c. (see jhāṛ-nā); a purge; a shower, continuous and heavy rain, downpour ( = jhaṛī)
subs: adv. Sweeping clean out, making a clean sweep, entirely
s.f. Clearing off (an account, &c.), full payment; finishing touch or stroke
v.n. To shower, to pour down, to rain without ceasing
s.f. Juggling, conjuring, exorcising; incantation, sorcery, hocus-pocus (particularly to cure a disease or the bite of a snake)
s.f. Sweeping, dusting, &c. ( = jhāṛ-poṅćh); sweepings; perquisites.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = jhājhā, q.v. s.m. An intoxicating mixture made of bhaṅg or hemp ( = gāṅjā).
more: n. m. 1. See jhājhā . 2. A kind of sieve. 3. (Bhoj. jhīsī ) Drizzling rain; drizzle.

n. m. 1. Sweeping; cleansing; clearing. 2. Exorcising; incantation. 3. Search (of the person). 4. A stool; evacuation; a purge. 5. Excrements; dirt; filth. jhāṛā phirnā, jhāṛe jhapṭe jānā , v. a. lit . To walk among the bushes; hence to ease one's self; to go to the rear ( pichhvāṛe jānā ). jhāṛā denā , v. n. 1. To submit to a search of one's person. 2. To exorcise; mutter incantations. jhāṛā lenā , v. n. To search (a person). jhāṛ pachhoṛ-kar dekhnā , v. a. To search thoroughly. jhāṛ poṅchh; jhāṛ jhūṛ, jhāṛ jhaṭak , n. f. Dusting; cleaning. jhāṛ phūṅk , n. f. Exorcising; incantations; sorcery. Jhāṛ phūṅk karvāu! davāī khiavalā se kā hou? Bhoj. Wom. Get an exorcist who will drive the evil spirit out of her. What is the good of medicine?

def: s.m. A kind of sieve; — (jhāra(nā)+S. कः), Drizzling rain, drizzle (syn. jhaṛī; jhīsī).
s.m. A bush, &c. = jhāṛ (used chiefly in comp.)
more: s. m. A stool, a purge. جهاڙاجهپٿي جانا jhāṛā jhapṭe jānā, To go to the necessary. جهاڙاجهپٿا لينا jhāṛā jhapṭā lenā, To search. جهاڙاپهرنا jhāṛā phirnā, or جهاڙاجهٿکاپهرنا jhāṛā jhaṭkā phirnā, To walk among the bushes (which means) to go to the necessary. جهاڙادينا jhāṛā denā, To submit to search. دوکان جهاڙا dūkān-jhāṛā, s. m. Sweepings of a (druggistʼs) shop, compound medicine.
subs: jhāṛe-jhapṭe jānā, jhāṛā-jhaṭkā phirnā, v.n. lit. 'To walk among the bushes'; to go to the necessary, to go to the rear, to ease oneself.

ety: rt. of jhāṛnā+ā = Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. Sweeping, cleaning, clearing; minute search (made by making a person suspected of stealing take off all his clothes); sweepings; a stool, purge, evacuation; excrement, dirt, filth; — conjuring, hocus-pocus, incantation
subs: (-ko), To submit to minute search (of one's person); to hocus-pocus, conjure, mutter incantations (over)
s.m. Sweepings of a (druggist's) shop; compound medicine.

def: adv. 1. In showers. 2. Without stopping; incessantly; heavily (raining); unceasingly. Uskī dukān par jhaṛā jhaṛ rupayā barastā hai .
(dakh.) To sweep out.
adv. Making a clean sweep, wholly, altogether.
adv. Hastily, rapidly.
s.m. Quickness, speed, haste, hurry
subs: adv. With all speed, quickly, post-haste, &c.

def: s.m. Quickness, speed, haste, hurry
subs: adv. With all speed, quickly, post-haste, &c.

def: s.m. = jharokhā, q.v. s.m. Loop-hole, eyelet-hole, lattice, window, casement, skylight; open door or arch (of a summer-house).
adv. = jhaṛā, q.v. s.m. (dialec.) = jhājhā, q.v. s.m. An intoxicating mixture made of bhaṅg or hemp ( = gāṅjā).

s.f. The falling off (of fruit from a tree, or of the hair of a man or animal, or of the feathers of a bird); a shower (of fruit, &c.); shavings, parings, sweepings, rubbish; small profits or savings; pickings, perquisites; snuff (of a candle).
more: (from جهڙنا) s. f. 1. The act of falling off (as, of fruit from the tree). 2. Snuff. 3. verb. n. of جهڙنا jhaṛnā, q. v.

def: n. m. 1. A duster; dishclout. 2. ( jhaṛan ) Sweepings; rubbish.
n. f. 1. Falling (of fruit from a tree); a shower (of fruit, etc.). 2. Sweepings; parings; shavings; small profits or savings. 3. Pickings; perquisites. 4. Snuff (of a candle).
(see next), Sweepings, rubbish ( = jhaṛan); — a coarse cloth for wiping furniture, &c., a duster, a dish-clout.
more: (from جهاڙنا) s. f. 1. Sweepings. 2. A coarse cloth for wiping furniture, &c.

def: v.t. To cause to be swept, to get (a place) swept; to get (a cloth, &c.) shaken or dusted; to cause to be exorcised.
more: (caus. of جهاڙنا) v. a. 1. To get swept. 2. To cause to be exorcised.

def: adv. See s.v. jhāṛ, 'sweeping.' s.m. A bush, shrub; bushes, bramble, brushwood, scrub, underwood, brake; a kind of arabesque work; a lustre or chandelier; a kind of firework
subs: s.m. Brambles, underwood; a tangled thicket, a brake
adj. (f. -ī), Like a bush or shrub; (in Bot.) cymose
s.m. Bush-thorn, bramble-thorn; (met.) a quarrelsome person
adj. & s.m. Full of brambles, bushy; — a forest, jungle; the forest of Baijnāth
v.n. To stick or cling (to one) like a bramble; to stick close (to one)

def: s.f. Heat, fieriness, pungency (as of pepper, &c.); flame, blaze; acrimony; contention, sparring, fight, cock-fight, bird-fight; — being possessed of a devil
more: s. f. Heat, fieriness (as, of pepper), acrimony, sparring, contention.
subs: s.f. Contention, sparring, fighting (particularly of birds).

def: n. f. 1. (Farrukh.) Flame; blaze. 2. Fieriness (as of cayenne). 3. W. Fight; cock-fight; bird-fight.
v.n. = jhaṛapnā, q.v.
more: v. n. To spar; fight (as cocks, etc.).

def: v.n. To spar, to fight (as cocks, &c.), to contend.
more: v. n. To spar, to fight (as, cocks, &c.), to contend.

def: s. f. (dakh.) Discharge (of a volley): see جهاڙن jhāṛan, or جهڙن jhaṛan.
s.m. = jhaṛotā, q.v. s.m. The end of the season (of fruit, &c.); — adj. Going out of season.
v.n. = jhar-barnā; and laṛ-baṛānā, qq.v. v.n. To burn furiously, to be all in flames, to be entirely burnt.
v.n. To burn furiously, to be all in flames, to be entirely burnt.
v.n. = jhar-barnā, q.v. v.n. To burn furiously, to be all in flames, to be entirely burnt.
s.m. = jhaṛ-ber, q.v.
more: s. f. (from جهاڙ A bush, and Sans. बदर see بير ber ) A wild ber- tree. (Jujube).

def: s.m. = jhaṛ-ber, q.v.
s.m. The fruit of the wild jujube tree or Zizyphus jujuba (see next).
more: s. m. (from جهاڙ A bush, and Sans. बदर see بير ber ) A wild ber- tree. (Jujube).

def: s.f. = jhaṛ-berī, q.v.
more: n. f. H. jhāṛī a bush, ber jujube. A small prickly bush which grows wild, bearing a small plum like the jujube; zizyphus jujuba , Wat.

def: [S. झटि+वदर+इका A wild ber or jujube bush, Zizyphus jujuba; the wild lote-tree.
more: s. f. (from جهاڙ A bush, and Sans. बदर see بير ber ) A wild ber- tree. (Jujube).

def: s.f. = jhāṛī, q.v. s.f. A pitcher with a long neck and a spout to it, an ewer.
s.f. A shrub, bush; bramble, brushwood, small bushes, underwood; copse, brake, thicket; wood, forest, jungle (see jhāṛ); — a jasmine bush or creeper
more: s. f. Forest, wood, underwood, brushwood, copse, bush, brake, shrub.

ety: rt. of jhaṛnā+Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Continued rain, showers; pickings, perquisites
more: s. f. 1. Continued rain, showers, storm, wet weather. 2. Perquisites.

def: s.f. A pitcher with a long neck and a spout to it, an ewer.
more: n. f. A pitcher with a long neck and a spout to it; an ewer.

n. f. 1. See jhāṛ (1.) 2. Forest; wood; jungle. jhāṛī būṭī kā ber , n. m. Wild plums ( jhaṛ-berī ). Jhāṛī būṅṭī ke haiṅ ber! ghūṅgaṭ-vālī ne toṛe haiṅ ber, albelī ne toṛe haiṅ ber! Delhi street cry. Lo! here behold these wild bush plums! by women veiled were broke these plums, by wantons gay were plucked these plums.
s. f. 1. A pitcher with a long neck and a spout to it, an ewer. 2. For H. جهاڙي jhāṛī, q. v.

def: s.f. A waterfall, cascade.
more: n. f. Showers; continued rain. Kar shor mor, bagle jhaṛyoṅ kā meṅh bulāveṅ, Pī pī kareṅ papīhe, meṅḍak malār gāveṅ . Nazīr. The peacock and the crane scream and rain-showers invoke, The cuckoo loving coos, the frogs their rain-songs croak. jhaṛī lagnā , v. n. To rain continually.

def: s.m. = jhajjhar, q.v. A large porous water-jar; a porous goglet ( = jhanjhar).
v.t. To shake, jerk, flap.
more: v. n. To shake, to jerk. ¶ jhiṛjhiṛānā, v. n. To rage, rail, storm.

def: s.f. Shaking (of a tree, &c.), flapping (of wings).
s. f. Mutual wrangling.
Jharkhand (a state in eastern India)

ety: jhār˚ = Prk. झाड(इ) or झाडे(इ) = S. शादय(ति), caus. of rt. शद्
def: v.t. To sift; to winnow (cf. jhāṛnā).
more: v. a. 1. To cause to drop or fall. 2. To sweep; brush; clean; dust. 3. To thrash; beat corn, etc. 4. To make a cut at one. Talvār kā hāth jhāṛā . 5. E. To comb. Sir jhāṛnā . 6. To knock or shake off; to shake. 1. Ber jhāṛnā . 2. Tumhārī ba-daulat do paise jhāṛ khāte haiṅ . 7. To exorcise; use incantations. 8. To break; break off; chip; clip. 9. To strike fire (as with flint). 10. To shed or lose (feathers). Par jhāṛnā . 11. To give a set-down ( āṛe hāth lenā 2-5.). jhāṛnā jhaṭaknā , v. n. To dust; clean. jhāṛ bāqī , n. f. 1. Full payment. 2. The finishing stroke.

ety: jhāṛ˚ = Prk. अज्झड्डे(इ) = S. अध्यर्दय(ति), caus. of rt. अर्द् with अधि
def: v.t. To clean, to sweep, to brush, to dust
more: v.a. To sweep, brush, shift, clean, strain, knock off, dash, strike, flap, strike fire (as, with a flint), to beat (bushes, &c.). جهاڙپچهورکرديکهنا jhāṛ pachhor kar dekhnā (dakh.) To try, to prove, to examine. جهاڙنا پهونکنا jhāṛnā phūnknā, To exorcise, to repeat spells or charms. جهاڙڐالنا jhāṛ ḍālnā, v. a. To sweep out or away. جهاڙدينا jhāṛ denā, v. a. To brush, to clear away.
subs: v.t. To sweep up, to clean
v.t. To look closely into, to search thoroughly, to examine minutely, to scrutinize
v.t. (intens.) To brush, brusn off, clean up, clear out, sweep away, sweep clean
v.t. (intens.) To sweep out, sweep away; to dust or clean well, &c.

ety: caus. of jhaṛnā; — jhāṛ˚ = Prk. झाड(इ) or झाडे(इ) = S. शादय(ति), rt. शद्
def: v.t. To cause to drop or fall; to strain; to shake (trees, carpets, &c.), to beat (bushes, &c.); to shake off, to cast (its feathers, as a bird); to brandish or flourish (a sword, stick, &c.), to make a cut (at); to give one a shaking or a set-down; to knock off, break off; to dash, strike, flap; to shed sparks, to strike out fire (as from a flint); to conjure, hocus-pocus, to repeat spells or charms over
subs: v.t. To exorcise, to repeat spells or charms over (a sick person).

def: v.n. = جهڙنا jhaṛnā, q.v. v.t. To sift; to winnow (cf. jhāṛnā).

ety: jhaṛ˚ = Prk. झड(इ) for छड(इ) = S. शद(ति), rt. शद्
def: v.n. To drop, fall, fall off (as fruit from a tree, hair from an animal, &c.); to be shed; to be poured; to be discharged (as a volley, or semen, &c.); to be shaken (as a cloth, &c.); to be winnowed, be sifted; to shake; to be sounded or played (as the naubat); to remain over, to be saved
more: v. n. To fall off (as, fruit from the tree), to be swept off, to shed, to emit, to drop, to fall, to pour, to shake, to strain, to be sounded (the نوبت), to be discharged (a volley).
subs: v.n. (intens.) To fall off, to drop off, to wither, to fade.

def: v.n. To be swept, be swept off, be swept clean; to be beaten or dusted (as clothes, carpets, &c.).

v.n. To flow or run in a slender stream, to trickle; to fall in drops, to drop; to ooze; to be shaken (as a cloth, &c.); to be winnowed, be sifted; — jharnā, s.m. A spring, a cascade, a jet dʼeau; a kind of sieve; a perforated skimmer.
more: n. m. S. र्निझर or झर a cascade, Pr. nichchhro; nijjharo . 1. A waterfall; jet d'eau ; cascade. 2. A kind of sieve.

v. n. 1. To ooze (water); trickle; leak. Jhireṅ hamāre nain, maram kā bhed na jāne koī, Apnī apnī kaho bhalā tum, merī akal kyoṅ khoī? The tears flow from my eyes, the heart's grief none doth know, Your own griefs tell you may, why me distract you so? 2. ( jharnā ). To be winnowed, sifted.

v. n. 1. To drop; fall (fruit); to be shed. Āṅdhī se sārā phal jhaṛ gayā . 2. To be swept clean. Makān jhaṛ gayā! 3. To have one's coat dusted; to be thrashed. 4. To be discharged ( semen ). 5. To be sounded, played (the naubat , q. v.). 6. To remain over. 7. Fac. To give. Chār paise jhaṛ rahte hain. jhaṛ'nī , ( Bot .) Deciduous.
1. s. m. A skimmer. 2. A spring, a cascade. 3. v. n. To spring, to flow. 4. See جهڙنا jhaṛnā.

def: adj. Falling off; (in Bot.) deciduous.
s.m. = jharokhā, q.v. s.m. Loop-hole, eyelet-hole, lattice, window, casement, skylight; open door or arch (of a summer-house).
more: n. m. S. जालक a window; H. jhirī an opening. 1. A window; lattice; casement; air-hole. 2. A summer house with open arches ( bārādarī ). Rām jharoke baiṭh-ke sab kā mujrā le; Jaisī jā kī chākrī vaisā wā ko de . Dohā.
s. m. (dakh.) Window: see جهروکها jharokhā.

def: s.m. Loop-hole, eyelet-hole, lattice, window, casement, skylight; open door or arch (of a summer-house).
more: s. m. A lattice, a window.

ety: rt. of jhaṛnā+Prk. वत्तअं or वट्टअं = S. वार्त्त+कं or वृत्त+कं
def: s.m. The end of the season (of fruit, &c.); — adj. Going out of season.
more: s. m. The end of the season (of fruit, &c.), going out of season.

ety: rt. of jhaṛnā+Prk. वत्तअं or वट्टअं = S. वार्त्त+कं or वृत्त+कं
def: s.m. The end of the season (of fruit, &c.); — adj. Going out of season.
s. f. Fighting (particularly, of birds), sparring, contention.
v. a. To cause to spar or fight; set by the ears. Logoṅ ko āpas meṅ jhaṛpāte ho. jhaṛpāyā jānā , v. n. To be affected by an evil eye or spirit ( jhapṭe meṅ ānā ).
v.t. To make (cocks or other birds) fight; to cause to spar or fight, to set by the ears; to take possession of, to blast (as an evil spirit).
more: (trans. of جهڙپنا) v. a. To fight (cocks or other birds), to make to spar or contend.

def: v.n. To be set a-fighting; to be set by the ears; to be possessed, be under the influence (of an evil spirit).
s.m. (dialec.) = jharnā, q.v. v.t. To sift; to winnow (cf. jhāṛnā).
s.m. The sound produced by the tearing of cloth ( = jhar).
n. m. The sound made in tearing new cloth.

ety: jha ṛ(nā)+tī = Prk. अत्तिआ = S. आप्तिका (आपि caus. augment+aff. ति+का)
def: Searching, &c. = jhāṛā, q.v.
subs: (-kī) = jhāṛā lenā. s.m. (dialec.) = jhājhā, q.v. s.m. An intoxicating mixture made of bhaṅg or hemp ( = gāṅjā).

def: v. a. (dakh.) To give or render an account.
v. a. (dakh. i. q. محاسبه لينا) To take an account.

ety: rt. of jhāṛnā 'to sweep,'+ū = Prk. इडओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m. A broom, a besom, a whisk; — a comet
more: s. m. f. A besom, broom, a comet. جهاڙوکش يا بردار jhāṛū-kash or -bardār, s. m. 1. A sweeper. 2. The cast of حلال خور ḥalāl-ḵẖor, q. v.
subs: v.n. To be swept, to be swept clean; to be cleaned out
v.t. To sweep, to clean; to make a clean sweep of all in a house, to spare nothing
v.t. To crush (one) down, to scold, chide; to castigate, chastise
s.m. A sweeper (by caste and occupation, syn. ḥalāl-ḵẖor, bhangī)
v.t. To beat with a broom.

def: n. f. 1. A broom. 2. A comet. Wom. Pair meṅ jhāṛū na māro, sūkhā lag jāëgā . Wom. Dont let the broom touch your foot or you'll have the wasting sickness (become as thin as a broomstick). jhāṛū phirnā , v. n. To be swept clean; ruined; swept away. Tere ghar meṅ Bīvī kī jhārū phir jāë ! Wom. curse. May you be swept out of house and home with Fatima's broom? jhāṛū denā , v. n. 1. To sweep; broom ( buhārnā ). 1. Ḍūbegā bhāṛū kā bhāṛū, rāt same ne de saë jhāṛū . Rus. Prov. You'll ruined be outright if sweep you do at night. 2. Rāt ke vaqt jhāṛū denī manhūs hai . Wom. It is unlucky to sweep at night. 2. To clean; cleanse. jhāṛū k . v. n. See āṛe hāth lenā (2 to 6.) under اَرَ fence. jhāṛū-kash , Ped. hybrid, n. m. A sweeper by caste and occupation. jhāṛū mārnā , v. a. To beat with a broom. Uske muṅh par jhāṛū māro! Broom-beat him on his mouth!
adj. 1. Wrinkled; faded; worn-out; decrepit; old. 2. Stupid; foolish; idiotic. Buḍḍhā jhaṛūs .
s.m. A kind of nutritious grass (the grain resembling that of shāmāḵẖ or Panicum frumentaceum, of which it is considered to be a wild species).
v.t. To cause to be shaken or dusted, to cause to be beaten; to have (a place) swept, &c.
more: (caus. of جهاڙنا) v. a. To cause others to sweep.

def: s.m. A fish; the sign Pisces
subs: s.m. The sign Pisces; — name of Kāmdev, the god of love.

def: s.m. An arbour, bower, place overgrown with creepers; a wood, thicket.

ety: S. झट्, perhaps for अध्यट; cf. झट्+इति
def: s.m. Quickness, celerity; — adj. Quick, brisk; — adv. Quickly, speedily, instantly, at once (cf. jhap)
more: adv. Quickly, instantly. جهٿ سي jhaṭ se, or جهٿ پٿ jhaṭ paṭ, adv. Quickly, hastily.

def: (dakh.) Quickly, hastily, at once.
adv. S. झटिति See jhap .
s.f. Jasmine, Jasminum auriculatum.
s.f. A jerk, wrench, shake; a toss, throw; a twitch, stitch (see next).
more: (from جهٿکنا) s. f. Twitch, stitch, toss, throw, jerk, shake.

ety: jhaṭak˚ = Prk. झटक्क(इ) or झटक्के(इ), fr. S. झट+कृ
def: v.t. To pull with a jerk, to jerk, touse, twitch; to snatch, snap, wrench; to shake (a cloth, &c.); — v.n. To be shaken, pulled down (as by sickness), to fall off (in flesh), to become lean or thin
more: 1. v. a. To touse, twitch, pull, jerk. 2. v. n. To become lean; to shake. جهٿک لينا jhaṭak lenā, To snatch.
v. a. To toss, to throw.
subs: v.t. To snatch or jerk away

def: v. a. 1. To jerk; pull violently. 2. To snap; snatch; wrench; take by force. Mele meṅ nath jhaṭak lī . 3. To dust; shake. jhaṭaknā, jhaṭak jānā , v. n. To become thin or lean; fall off. Chār din ke bukhār meṅ bachchā jhaṭak gayā . Wom. jhaṭke kā māl , n. m. Booty; plunder; loot.
s.f. Squall, driving rain; gust; shock; clap, flap.
more: s. Flap, clap, rain blown with a strong wind. See بوچهار bauchhār.

def: E. n. f. Driving rain.
s.m. A small tree, a shrub, bush (see jhāṛ).
s.f. The plant Flacourtia cataphracta.
adv. See jhaṭ.

Yeh jo kaṛve ho-ke ham ko ab jhiṛakte haiṅ yahāṅ, Inkī talkhi meṅ hazāroṅ haiṅ bharī shīrīniyāṅ; Uṭh sake jab tak uṭhā, aë dil, tu inkī sakhtiyāṅ, Lazzateṅ jannat ke mevoṅ kī bahut hoṅgī wahāṅ; Par yeh mīṭhī gāliyāṅ khūbāṅ kī khānī phir kahāṅ? Offended she now scolds me in her heat, Her gall's for me a hundred times more sweet, Bear up, O heart, as canst, though harsh she treat Thee, for though Heaven o'erflows with joys complete, No sweet lips' sweet abuse thee there will greet.

more: n. f. 1. Cleft; crevice; chink; fissure; opening; leak. 2. A small spring dug in a nālā , where water percolates a few feet below the surface. 3. Blight. jhirī meṅ se jhāṅknā , v. n. To peep through a key-hole.

def: s.f. = jhillī, q.v. s.f. A thin skin, a pellicle, the inner skin (of the stomach, &c.), a membrane, the omentum or caul; the inner bark (of a tree); parchment.
adj. Running in a slender stream (water, &c.), trickling; splashing.
more: adj. Running in a slender stream (water). jhirjhir'ā , adj. Thin (as cloth).
adj. Running in a slender stream (water, &c.).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Worn, thin, emaciated; thin, flimsy, badly woven (cloth).

ety: jhirjhir, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To run or flow in a small stream, to trickle.
more: v. n. To trickle; rill; flow in a thin stream.
v. a. 1. To shake (a tree); jerk; flap; worry (a rat). 2. To give one a good shaking or setting down ( āṛe hāth lenā ). Aisā jhaṛjhaṛāyā, ki nok dum bhāgā . Slang.
v. n. To trickle, to rill.

ety: a var. form of the preceding
def: v.n. To rage, rail, storm, bluster.
s.f. Chiding, scolding; petulance, snappishness; rage, bluster.
more: (from جهڙجهڙانا jhiṛjhiṛ-ānā or جهڙجهڙنا) s. f. (dakh.) Chiding.

def: v.t. = jhiṛaknā, q.v.; and = jhiṛakwānā, q.v. v.t. To shake, jerk; to drive away, beat off, put aside or away; to snap at, to scold, rebuke, chide, threaten, browbeat
more: v. n. (dakh.) To scold, to jerk, to speak snappishly, to snub, to check, to blame, to chide.
subs: v.t To shake or jerk off; to drive away, beat off, &c.

def: (from جهڙکنا) s. (dakh.) Scolding, snubbing ( Harris ).
n. f. Snappishness; scolding.
s.f. Jerk; repercussion; snapping; snappishness; frowning; threat; scolding, rebuke
more: s. f. Rebuff, snappishness, frowning, jerk, repercussion.
subs: jhiṛkiyāṅ denā, v.t. To snap at, to chide, scold, rebuke, threaten.

def: s.f. Mist-like rain, misle, drizzle, shower.
s.m. A fight, a quarrel, &c. = jūjh
s.m. A stomach. paunch; a pendulous belly; nest (of a bird, = jhūṅjh or jhoṅjh).
n. m. 1. A nest ( ghoṅslā ). 2. A paunch ( toṅd ). jhojh'ā ; Tir. dhodhīab , n. m. A pot bellied person ( peṭal ).
more: s. m. 1. A nest. 2. The stomach. 3. A pendulous belly.

def: s.m. A paunch ( = jhojh); — a pot-bellied person, a corpulent man; — name of an inferior class of Musalmāns in the Doab, &c. (chiefly converts from Hinduism).
adj. Cracked (as a voice, sound, &c.).

s.m. A kind of grass (to which camels are very partial, and which is occasionally given as fodder to horned cattle. It grows to the height of about two feet, and is known also by the name of janglī nīl, 'wild indigo').
more: n. m. A grass to which camels are very partial, and which is occasionally given as fodder to horned cattle. It is also called jaṅglī nīl or wild indigo.

def: adj. = jhojhrā, q.v. adj. Cracked (as a voice, sound, &c.).

s.m. (in H., but) f. (in Urdū), Inclination, leaning over, deviation (from perpendicularity), deflexion; bend, incurvation; dipping (of a scale), preponderance, weight; (in Mech.) sensibility (of a balauce); breeze, current (of air), gust, blast, violent sweep (of a sudden gust), impulse (of a squall, &c.); influence of drink or intoxication, &c.); swoop (of a bird of prey); shock, impetus, a shove or push (in swinging); blow, collision; rolling, reeling, staggering
more: n. f. 1. Bend; inclination; deflexion; bulge; curve. 2. ( Mech .) Sensibility (of a balance). 3. Reeling; staggering; rolling; bobbing. 4. The fumes of liquor. Nashe kī jhok meṅ baktā hai. jhok chiṭṭhī , n. f. A fraudulent note of hand, cheque, or bill. jhok saṅbhālnā , v. n. 1. To keep straight (a bamboo, etc.). 2. To endure a loss. jhok mārnā, ṭāl mārnā , v. n. To give a twist to the beam of a scale ( ḍanḍī mārnā ).
s. f. A jolt, a puff or gust.
subs: s.f. An empty note or paper, a fraudulent note of hand, or cheque, or bill, &c.
(-kā, or -kī), To withstand or bear up against a gust (of wind, as a tree, &c.), to bear the shock (of), to weather (a storm); to endure (a loss)
To receive a shock or blow, to be displaced, to roll, &c.
To give a (fraudulent) twist or turn (to the beam of a scale).

def: s.m. Breeze, current (of wind), puff, gust, blast; contact, collision, blow, shock ( = jhok); swaying, swinging, swing, oscillation; fraudulent turn (of a balance, in weighing).
more: s. m. A blow, a contact, collision, a gust, blast, breeze or current (of wind).
s. m. (dakh.) Bunch (of grapes, &c.): see جهونکا jhoṅkā.

ety: rt. of jhok-nā, 'to throw'+iʼā = iwā = Prk. इअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. The thrower of fuel (into an oven, or a furnace), fireman ( = jhokiyā, q.v.).
s.f. The spot on which the fireman stands to feed the fire of a furnace, &c.; the mouth of a furnace.
more: n. f. The mouth of the furnace.

def: s.m. One who supplies fuel to a furnace, a fireman ( = jhoṅkiyā; jhokā).

ety: jhok˚ = Prk. झेवक्क(इ), fr. S. क्षेप (or क्षप)+कृ
def: v.t. To throw, cast, toss; to toss down, pour down, quaff, guzzle, swill; to put (in); to supply (fuel, to a furnace or oven), to heat (an oven or a furnace); to set fire to (straw, &c.); — v.n. To sway, roll, reel, stagger; — s.m. Fuel (for heating a furnace or an oven)
subs: v.t. (intens.) To throw away (in or on, meṅ), to waste, squander (money, lives, &c.); to risk, hazard, put at stake; to set fire to (straw, &c.); to toss down, &c. ( = jhoknā).

def: v.t. To cause to incline or bend; to bring (a gun) into position (to fire), to point (a gun); to aim (at, -par), direct (speech at = jhukhānā); to bear down upon; to swoop down on.
more: v. a. See jukhānā 3, 5. 1. To bear upon; come down on (a paper kite). 2. To put in; pour; feed a fire. 3. To throw away; lavish ( uṛānā , 11). Sārā rupayā jūë meṅ jhok dīyā . 4. To risk; hazard; stake. Jān jhok dī .
v. a. (dakh.) To throw, to cast.

def: s.m. Puckering or bagging (as of ill-made clothes), bagginess, rumple, pucker, crease; incurvation or contraction (of a limb, &c.), palsy ( = jholā) ; — a brood, hatch; a litter, farrow; a birth; a batch (of eggs); — gravy, broth, soup ( = jūsh); — a wash of gold, gilding (syn. mulammā)
more: n. m. 1. Rumple; crease; pucker. 2. Gilding ( mulammā ). 3. A litter; brood; furrow; hatch; a birth. 4. Gravy ( rasā ). Hin. jhol phernā, chaṛhānā, yā karnā , v. n. To gild ( mulammā phernā ). jhol jhāl , n. m. 1. Delay ( der ). 2. Quarrel ( jhagṛā ); contention; dispute; altercation. jhol-dār , adj. 1. Rumpled. 2. Gilded. 3. Having a gravy. jhol ḍālnā , v. a. To crease; rumple; pucker. jhol nikālnā , v. a. 1. To take out the creases; to smooth. 2. To bring forth; give birth.
s. m, 1. Puckering or wrinkling (as, of ill made clothes). 2. A litter, brood, farrow, hatch, a birth.
subs: (-par), To put a wash of gold (on), to gild
s.m. Shaking, scuffle, squabble, altercation, quarrel, row; delay
adj. Puckered, rumpled, bagging, baggy; — having a gravy; — gilded
(-meṅ), To make baggy, to pucker, rumple, crease
v.n. To take the puckering or bagginess (out of, -meṅ-se), to remove a rumple or crease (from), to smooth; to raise a brood, to hatch; to litter; to bring forth, give birth (to).

def: s.m. Bagging, bellying out; pendulousness; incurvation; curve (as of a rope hanging freely between two points of suspension); a bag, wallet, knapsack; bending or waving (the hand, in beckoning), beckoning (with the hand); — palsy, paralysis; — a hot wind or blast; a stroke of the sun; a cold blighting wind (which withers or dries up the ears of wheat); — adj. Loose, slack, bagging, baggy; pendulous; curved.
more: n. m. 1. The depression, bend or curve of a rope. 2. Beckoning with the hand ( jhālā ). 3. A knapsack; wallet; bag. 4. A cold wind which dries up the ears of wheat. 5. Palsy; paralysis. 6. Hot wind or blast ( lū ). Āmoṅ ko jholā mār gayā. jholā , adj. Loose; slack. Khāṭ jholā ho gaī .
s. m. 1. A stroke of the sun. 2. The palsy. 3. A knapsack, a wallet.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit [Term?], related to *झुल्यति (*jhulyati, “to swing”).

ety: S. झौलिका; prob. a Prk. form fr. rt. व्हल्
def: s.f. A bag, sack, pouch, wallet; — pendulous belly (of an ox, &c.); — a sheet used as a fan in winnowing ( = jhūlī, q.v.); a trick in wrestling
more: n. f. jholā , n. m. 1. A wallet; sack; bag. 1. Faqīr kī jholī meṅ sab kuchh . Prov. A mendicant's bag contains all sorts of things. (He can work miracles). 2. Shokhī na kar, havvā jholī meṅ ḍāl-kar le jāëgā . Wom. Dont be naughty or the ogre will come and carry you off in his bag. (Said to frighten a child into quietness). 2. ( jhūlī ) A sheet of cloth waved to and fro as a fan for winnowing grain when there is no wind. 3. The belly of an ox. 4. A trick in wrestling. jholī chhoṛnā , v. n. To grow fat; to become pot-bellied. Jholī chhoṛ dī hai .
subs: v.n. To get or have a pendulous belly, to grow fat
adj. Furnished with a bag or pouch; (in Bot. and Anat.) saccate.

def: adj. ( Bot . & Anat ). Saccate.

ety: jhol˚ = S. व्हालय(ति), rt. व्हल्; see jhūlnā
def: v.t. To move or swing to and fro (as a fan, &c.); to shake, put in motion, stir, agitate (as water, &c.); — s.m. A bag ( = jholā, jholī).
more: v. a. S. दोलन from दुल to oscillate. 1. To fan. Pankhā jholnā . 2. To shake; stir; agitate. Tulsī, aisī pīt kar jaise bhor-talā; Jhol jhāl-kī pī līyā, pher lagā galā . Dohā Like pond's green scum, O Tulsī, your love be, Parted to drink, again the parts close lie.

def: v.t. To cause to be soldered or cemented, to get (a thing) soldered or repaired.
v.t. (dialec.), To set in motion or action, to set agoing, to get up; to apply; to beat, strike.
more: v. n. 1. To begin; set agoing; set in motion. 1. Chakkī jhonā . 2. Nānas (nālish) jhonā . Rus. 2. To beat; strike. Rus. Ek laṭh jhoyā. jhagṛā jhonā , v. a. To make a long tiresome story. Isā jhagṛā jhoyā, jī bākal gayā . Rus.

def: s.m. Name of a division of the Kurmī tribe.
s.m. = jhojh, q.v. s.m. A fight, a quarrel, &c. = jūjh
s.f. (dialec.) = jhaunjh, q.v. s.f. (dialec.) Itching
more: n. f. H. jhaurnā to claw. Itching ( chul ). jhauṅjh mārnā , v. n. To itch.

def: s.f. = jhok, q.v. s.m. (in H., but) f. (in Urdū), Inclination, leaning over, deviation (from perpendicularity), deflexion; bend, incurvation; dipping (of a scale), preponderance, weight; (in Mech.) sensibility (of a balauce); breeze, current (of air), gust, blast, violent sweep (of a sudden gust), impulse (of a squall, &c.); influence of drink or intoxication, &c.); swoop (of a bird of prey); shock, impetus, a shove or push (in swinging); blow, collision; rolling, reeling, staggering
more: s. f. 1. A shove or push in swinging. 2. The impulse of a squall or sudden blast of wind. جهونک دينا jhonk denā, 1. To set fire to (particularly, straw, &c.). 2. To throw dust, &c. 3. To throw away (the lives of men uselessly). جهونک کهانا jhonk khānā, To roll.
s. (dakh.) A leech ( Harris ): see جونک joṅk.
subs: s.f. An empty note or paper, a fraudulent note of hand, or cheque, or bill, &c.

To receive a shock or blow, to be displaced, to roll, &c.

def: s.m. = jhokā, q.v.; — (also) bunch or cluster (of fruit); ear or beard (of corn).
more: s. m. (dakh.) 1. See جهوکا jhokā. 2. Ear or beard (of corn), bunch of grapes: ( Harris ).

def: v.t. = jhukānā, jhoṅknā, q.v. v.t. To cause to stoop, to bend downwards, to tilt; to cause to nod; to incline (the head), to nod, bow; to abash; to lower (as a gun, in order to take aim); to put into a scale, to weigh; to speak (at), direct speech at, -par)
s.m. The keeping safe of property; — the charge for securing property from accidents, insurance ( = jokhim; bīmā).
s.m. = jhokiyā, q.v. s.m. One who supplies fuel to a furnace, a fireman ( = jhoṅkiyā; jhokā).

v.t. = jhoknā, q.v. v.t. To cause to incline or bend; to bring (a gun) into position (to fire), to point (a gun); to aim (at, -par), direct (speech at = jhukhānā); to bear down upon; to swoop down on.

ety: Prk. झुंपओ; S. क्षुम्प+कः; cf. khoṅpā
def: s.m. A cluster or bunch (of fruit), a spike; a coil (of hair on the top of the head), top-knot ( = jhoṅṭā); — a cloth with eyelets which covers the whole body.
more: s. m. 1. A bunch of fruit. 2. A cloth with eyelets which covers the whole body. 3. A spike.

def: n. m. jhoṅpṛī , n. f. H. jhāṅpnā to cover. 1. A hut; shed. 1. Rahe jhoṅpṛe meṅ, khuāb dekhe mahloṅ kā . Prov. He lives in a hut and dreams of palaces. 2. Tan lagī dhupṛī, balā chhāë jhupṛī . Prov. His back warmed by the sun, who cares a thatch to put on? (When the rain came down his thatch he would renew, when the sun came out a thatch without he'd do). 3. Apne jhoṅpṛe kī khair māṅgo! Prov. Pray for the welfare of your own hut! (Keep your breath to cool your ain porridge). 4. Āī mauj faqīr kī, dīyā jhoṅpṛā phūṅk . Prov. With holy frenzy the ascetic's soul inspired, his earthly dwelling with his own hands fired. 2. Wom. The hairs of the head. Jhoṅpṛā baṛhāë phiṛe hai .

ety: Prk. झुंपडओ, and झुपडिआ; S. झुम्प+र+क and इका
def: s.f. (dim.) [Prk. झुंपडओ, and झुपडिआ; S. झुम्प+र+क and इका], A small hut or hovel built of reeds and mud; cottage, cot, hut; a thatched roof or shed.
more: s. m. A cottage, a hut.

ety: Prk. झुंपडओ, and झुपडिआ; S. झुम्प+र+क and इका
def: s.f. (dim.) = H جهونپڙا झोंपड़ा jhoṅpṛā, s.m. [Prk. झुंपडओ, and झुपडिआ; S. झुम्प+र+क and इका], A small hut or hovel built of reeds and mud; cottage, cot, hut; a thatched roof or shed.
more: s. f. A cottage, a hut.

def: n. f. A small shed in the fields to shelter the man who watches the crops.

s.m. A bunch or cluster (of fruit, = jhoṅpā); — also = jholā, q.v. s.m. A cluster or bunch (of fruit), a spike; a coil (of hair on the top of the head), top-knot ( = jhoṅṭā); — a cloth with eyelets which covers the whole body.

ety: S. झुण्ट+कः, or जुटकः; rt. जुट्
def: s.m. Coil (of a woman's hair), top-knot ( = jhoṅpā; khoṅpā); a buffalo (so called on account of its hump); — swing, oscillation; the motion of a swing; nodding; — jhoṅṭā denā (-ko), To shake (the head); to nod
more: s. m. 1. The hair of the back part of the head. (Sans. जटा Hair matted). 2. The motion of a swing. جهونٿا دينا jhonṭā denā, To shake oneʼs head with violence.
subs: jhoṭam-jhāṭā, s.m. Mutual pulling and tugging at the hair of the head.

def: s.f. Coil (of a woman's hair); — a young buffalo.
s.m. = H جهوپڙي झोपड़ी jhopṛī, s.f. See jhoṅpṛā, and jhoṅpṛī.
s.f. = H جهوپڙا झोपड़ा jhopṛā, s.m. See jhoṅpṛā, and jhoṅpṛī.
s.m. The haulm or stalk of a leguminous plant (such as mūṅg, moṭh, &c. — used as fodder).
more: n. 1. The stalks of leguminous plants, as mūṅg and moṭh , used as fodder. 2. See jhojh , 2.

def: v.t. To shake (fruit off a tree); to beat, to thrash ( = jhārnā); — to pound, grind (cf. jhauṛnā & jholnā).
more: v. n. To shake fruit from a tree.

def: n. f. A narrow lane between two walls.
more: n. m. S. कूट A falsehood; a lie. 1. Jhūṭ kī nāo maṅj-dhār meṅ ḍūbtī hai . Prov. Falsehood's freight sinks midstream. 2. Sach kahe so mārā jāë, Jhūṭ kahe so laḍḍū khāë . Prov. The truthful starve and die while liars dainties sweet enjoy. jhūṭ bolnā , v. n. To lie; tell a falsehood. Jhūṭ bolnā khe khānā barābar hai . Prov. To tell a lie is to eat dirt. jhūṭ pakaṛnā , v. n. To detect or convict one of a falsehood. jhūṭ jānnā , v. n. To disbelieve; discredit; distrust. jhūṭ sach , n. m. jhūṭī sachchī , n. f. Misrepresentation; invention; fabrication; fable. jhūṭ sach lagānā , v. a. 1. To misrepresent; romance; to poison one's ear. 2. To speak against; backbite. jhūṭ kī poṭ , n. f. A bundle of lies. jhūṭ mūṭ , adv. In jest; for nothing at all. Ham ne to jhūṭ mūṭ kahā thā, tum sach hī jān gaë. jhūṭoṅ pūchhnā , v. a. To ask one as a matter of form or civility.

n. f. S. जुष्ट the remains of a meal. 1. Leavings; crumbs. 1. Jhūṭ kūṭ ke tukṛoṅ par guzrān kartī hai . Wom. She just manages to live on scraps and leavings. 2. Jhūṭ chhoṛnī burī hai . Wom. It is unlucky to leave any thing on your plate. 2. Pollution; defilement. Chauke meṅ jhūṭ ho gaī. jhūṭ khāne-vālā, jhūṭ-khānā , n. m. One who eats the leavings of another. jhūṭ khānā , v. n. To eat one's leavings.

def: s.m. = jhoṅṭā, q.v. s.m. Coil (of a woman's hair), top-knot ( = jhoṅpā; khoṅpā); a buffalo (so called on account of its hump); — swing, oscillation; the motion of a swing; nodding; — jhoṅṭā denā (-ko), To shake (the head); to nod
subs: jhoṭam-jhāṭā, s.m. Mutual pulling and tugging at the hair of the head.
ex: یہ دھوکے باز نے ہَمیں جُھوٹی مَعلُومات دی۔
ye dhoke bāz ne hamẽ jhūṭī ma'lūmāt dī.
This fraud gave us false information.
تھانے دار نے غَدّار سے جُھوٹے دَسْتاویز پَکَڑ لِئے۔
thāne dār ne ġaddār se jhūṭe dastāvez pakaṛ lie.
The policeman seized counterfeit documents from the traitor.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit जुष्ट (juṣṭa). Compare Punjabi جُھوٹھا /ਝੂਠਾ (jhūṭhā).

def: s.m. = jhūpā; ḍhūʼā, qq.v. s.m. A collection, heap, pile (as of fruit, corn-stalks, &c.; cf. ḍhūʼā).
more: n. m. A pile of corn-stalks.

def: adj. & s.m. Unoccupied, idle; — one who wastes time, an idler, a trifler.
s.m. = jhūʼā, q.v. s.m. = jhūpā; ḍhūʼā, qq.v. s.m. A collection, heap, pile (as of fruit, corn-stalks, &c.; cf. ḍhūʼā).
v.n. = jhuṅjhlānā, q.v. v.n. To be petulant or irritable, to snap (at); to rage, storm; to be peevish or fretful.
v.n. = jūjhnā, q.v. v.n. To fight, to make war.
adj. (f. -ī), Bent, stooping, inclined; (in Bot.) decumbent

ety: caus. of jhuknā; — jhuk˚+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि;+n ā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To cause to stoop, to bend downwards, to tilt; to cause to nod; to incline (the head), to nod, bow; to abash; to lower (as a gun, in order to take aim); to put into a scale, to weigh; to speak (at), direct speech at, -par)
more: v. a. 1. To cause to bend downwards. 2. To incline the head; nod; bow. Sir jhukānā . 3. To take an aim; to point (a gun). 4. To pour; give. Ser bhar chīnī jhukā do . 5. To speak at one; to use inuendoes. Ham par bhī jhukāī .
(caus. of جهکنا jhuknā ) v. a. 1. To cause to stoop, to tilt, bend downwards, to bow, incline. 2. To nod or make nod.

ety: jhukā(nā)+āʼo or āw = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: s.m. The state of being bent downward; stoop; bend, inclination; curve; irregular line; (in Mech.) flexibility
more: n. m. 1. The state of being bent downward; stoop; inclination. 2. ( Mech .) Flexibility. jhukāo khet , ( Mech .) An inclined plane. jhukāo lachak . n. ( Phys .) Elasticity of flexure.
(from جهکنا jhuknā ) s. m. The state of being bent downward, stoop.

def: s.f. Bending downwards, stooping; nodding; the state of being bent downward; bend; inclination.
more: s. f. Stooping, nodding, bending downwards.

ety: jhuk˚ = Prk. झुक्क(इ), fr. S. क्षुभ (acc. sing. n. क्षुप)+कृ
def: v.n. To be bent down (as the bough of a tree), to bend, stoop, lean, incline; to be tilted; to preponderate, to dip (as a scale, or the arm of a balance); to bow, to nod; to crouch; to be prone (to, -meṅ), be ready (for); to hang down, to drop (as the eyes); to be abashed; to stoop (to, -se), pay homage (to), humble oneself (before); to submit, to yield; to be angry; to be perplexed; to be spent, be thrown away (in or on, -meṅ)
more: v. n. 1. To bend, or be bent down; to incline; lean; stoop to. 1. Ūṅṭ kī chorī, aur jhuke jhuke! Prov. Steal a camel and creep! (The thief all fours may go, big thefts are not hid so). 2. Bāṅs baṛhe jhuk jāë, aranḍ baṛhe ṭūṭ jāë . Prov. The bamboo grows and bends, the castor breaks. 2. To stoop; bow; kneel to. Jhuke āp se us se jhuk jāiye, Ruke āp se us se ruk jāiye! Mīr Hasan. 3. To drop; hang down. Nīṅd ke māre āṅkheṅ jhukī ātī haiṅ . 4. To pay homage or respect. 5. To be prone to; have an inkling for. Woh har kām meṅ jhuk paṛtā hai . 6. To yield; give way; surrender. 7. To be spent, lavished, wasted. Sārā rupaiyā isī meṅ jhuk gayā . 8. To be thrown into the fire. jhukā sikhar , n. f. ( Math .) An ellipse. jhuk'ī , ( Bot .) Decumbent ( decumbens ). jhukī-huī , adj. Bending down; drooping.
v. n. 1. To nod, to bow, to stoop, to be bent, especially downwards (as, the bough of a tree), to be tilted. 2. To be angry, to be perplexed.
subs: jhuk-jānā, v.n. intensives of and = jhuknā.
wety: Cognate with Nepali झुक्नु (jhuknu), Punjabi ਝੁਕਣਾ (jhukṇā) / جھُکݨا (jhukṇā), Marathi झुकणे (jhukṇe).

def: s.f. Affliction, suffering, grief, sorrow.
more: n. f. Affliction; grief; sorrow; suffering.
s. f. (dakh. i. q. غم خوري) Affliction, grief, sorrow.

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Bending, stooping, &c. (see jhuknā)
subs: adj. & adv. Crouching and dodging (to escape observation, &c.).

def: s.f. A swing ( = jhūlā; jhūlnā); body-clothes (of cattle, &c.), horse-cloth, housings ( = A. jul); a bag, wallet ( = jholā).
more: n. f. A. jul . See jhūlā (1). Body-clothes of cattle, etc.; trappings; housings. Khārishtī kutyā aur makhmal kī jhūl! Prov. A mangy slut and a velvet coat! (An ugly woman, a handsome dress.)!
(corrupt. of جل jull ) s. f. Bodyclothes of cattle, housings, a bag, a wallet, a knapsack.

def: s.m. A swinging-rope, a swing; a cradle; — a species of song sung when swinging
more: n. m. 1. A puff or gust of wind; a blow; blast. Dam bulbul-i-garīb kā tan se nikal gayā, Jhokā nasīm kā joṅ hī san se nikal gayā . Nāsikh. 2. ( bhāṛ-jhokā ) One who feeds the fire; a fireman.

n. m. jhūl , n. f. S. दुल to swing. 1. A swing. Ab ritu āī, saiyāṅ, jhuluā jhulan kī, Chiṭhyā na bhejī, saiyāṅ, gharvā avan kī. Barsātī . Rain song. 2. A class of songs sung when swinging. jhūlā jhūlnā , v. n. To swing. jhūlā ḍālnā , v. a. To put up a swing.
(see جهولنا jhūlnā ) s. m. A swing, the rope on which people swing.
subs: (-meṅ), To put up a swinging-rope or a swing.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit [Term?], related to *झुल्यति (*jhulyati, “to swing”).

def: s.f. Swinging, oscillating, &c.
subs: (cf. S. dolana+yātrā), s.f. A Hindū festival kept about new moon in August in honour of Kr̤ishṇa and his mistress Rādhā. (Idols representing these personages are placed in a cradle or chair suspended from the roof of the temple or house and swung to and fro while offerings of fruits, &c. are presented. The Brahmans are feasted, and love-songs of the most indecent description are chanted all night. This festival is usually celebrated for five days and nights.)

def: v.t. To cause to swing or sway; to swing, to push a swing (in which others are), to give (one) a swing; to rock (a cradle); to make (one, as a dun) dance after one; to dangle, hang
more: v. a. 1. To shove a swing in which others are seated; to rock. Jhulā, morī sainī, Shām jhulat palnā! Song. Rock the cradle, sister mine! Shām in the cradle rocks. 2. To make a dun, etc. dance after one. 3. To keep one a long time over any thing. Ek hī kitāb meṅ chhaë mahīne se jhulā rakkhā hai .
(caus. of جهولنا) v. a. 1. To swing, to shove a swing in which others are, to rock (a cradle). 2. To dangle, to hang. 3. To make another (as a dun) dance after one.
subs: v.t. To keep one running to and fro; to keep one (at, -meṅ, a thing, as reading, &c.).

def: s.m. Swinging, waving, swaying from side to side; wavy motion (as of trees, water, &c.), oscillation.
more: n. m. A waving motion (of water, trees); oscillation.

def: v. a. To sear, to singe.
v. n. To be scorched or singed.

v.n. To be burnt, be scorched, be singed, be charred, be parched, be seared, be branded ( = bhulasnā); — v.t. To scorch, singe, &c.; (met.) to blacken (the face), to disgrace (by getting one to accept a bribe, &c.)
more: v. n. To be charred, singed, burned, branded. Roṭī jhulas gaī. jhulasnā, bhulasnā, jhulas denā , v. a. 1. To char; burn. 2. ( Met .) To blacken one's face; to bribe. 3. To give unwillingly; to get rid of a troublesome fellow by giving him something. Bāre mastoṅ ne hoshyārī kī, De-ke kuchh mohtasib kā muṅh jhulsā . Mīr. At length the wassailers this cute device hit on, A sop they gave, th' inquistor a brand fixed on.
v. n. To be singed, scorched or seared.
subs: v.t. To scorch, burn, singe, &c. = jhulsānā.

def: s. (dakh.) See چين chīn.
Syed Muhammad Usman Marwandi; a Sufi philosopher-poet from Afghanistan buried in modern-day Sindh
ex: توں ایں میرا لجپال، جی سیہونی لال
You are my preserver-of-honour, oh the red one of Sehwan.
wety: From لال (lāl, “red”) denoting the red attire worn by him.

def: s.f. A cloth or sheet waved to and fro as a fan for winnowing grain when there is no wind ( = jholī); — a swinging oot, a hammock.
v.t. To burn, to scorch, to singe ( = jhulasnā).
more: (s. ज्वलका Flame) v. a. To scorch, to singe (as, the small feathers of a plucked fowl).

def: s.m. A shirt or frock that covers the body from the shoulders downward (syn. jhangā).
more: (see جهول jhūl, corrupt. of جل jull ) s. m. A shirt or vest that covers the body from the shoulder downwards.

def: s.f. A wrinkle, fold, plait (in cloth), a crease.

s.m. = jhurmaṭ, q.v. s.m. A dense mass, a crowd, multitude, large gathering or muster, assembly, ball; array (of troops), manœuvres (of troops); battle, conflict; a shawl twisted round the head as a turban.
v.n. = jhūlnā, q.v.
more: v. n. To swing, to dangle.

ety: jhūl˚ = S. व्हल(ति), rt. व्हल् (orig. ध्वल्
def: v.n. To hang down, to dangle; to swing, sway to and fro, to oscillate, rock, reel; to swing (for amusement or exercise); — s.m. A swing; a cradle; a kind of poem or song.
more: v. n. 1. To swing; dangle; oscillate; rock. 1. Kālā chiṛā aṭambar jhūle, An khāve, nā pānī bhūle . Riddle. Ans. Dol . A bucket. 2. Jhūlūṅgī nāṅh hiṅḍole māṅ, Jhūlat jīā ḍarat sai merā; Nikle jān jhakole māṅ . W. Rus. song. In merry-go-round I will not swing, With fear all o'er I'm quivering, My heart unto my mouth doth cling. 3. Piyā hote tau maiṅ kham gaṛātī; Saiyāṅ hote tau ḍorī mangātī; Shauq raṅg, ab mujhe chūṅdrī kaun raṅgā de? Kaho jhūlā jhūlūṅ maiṅ kis raṅg? Kaho pag jorūṅ maiṅ kis saṅg? Merī birhā jalāī umaṅg, mere pīyā ko kaun bulā de? Malhār song. Had but my love been here a swing frame had been mine, My husband here, swing ropes for me he would entwine, I've no one here a bright gay dress to dye for me! How could I, say, join in the pleasures of the swing? With whom foot pressed to foot the swing make to upspring? Joy's spring's dried up, no one to bring my love to me! 2. To b…
1. v. n. To swing (for exercise). 2. To swing, to dangle, to hang, to oscillate. 3. s. m. A kind of poem.

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Scorched, singed, charred, seared, branded.
more: Wom. adj. Singed; charred; burnt; seared; branded. Maiṅ is jhulse ko kahāṅ se khāne ko dūṅ? Wom. Whence am I to feed this branded (earn-nothing) wretch?

def: v.t. To cause to singe; to have singed; — to scorch, to singe, to sear, to brand.
more: Caus. of جهلسنا To cause to singe, seorch, sear.
(causal of جهلسنا) v. a. To cause to singe, to singe, sear, scorch.

ety: rt. of jhūl-nā+S. अ+कः (with व inserted)
def: s.m. A wreath or garland of flowers.
s.m. Swaying, waving, undulating; abundance of foliage
more: s. m. 1. Abundance of foliage. 2. Waving, undulating. جهوم جهوم jhūm jhūm, Gathering of the clouds.
subs: s.m. A gathering of clouds; — adv. Crowding, in dense crowds, thickly; overpoweringly (as sleep, &c.)

def: s.f. A dense mass, a crowd, multitude, an assembly, company, a ball; array (of troops); battle, engagement ( = jhurmaṭ).
more: s. f. An assembly, a ball; ( met. ) a battle, an engagement.

def: s.m. A gathering, assembly, company, band; a company of women dancing; a kind of dance (in which the dancers join hands, syn. dast-band); a kind of song sung by women in concert; — an ornament consisting of a number of chains forming a fringe, which is attached to the top-knot (of a woman's head) and falls on the forehead.
more: n. m. S. घूर्ण to move to and fro. 1. A company; band. 2. A company of women dancing. 3. A kind of song sung by women; a concert. 4. An ornament worn by women. It hangs from the topknot over the forehead.

def: s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode (cf. jhūmar, and jhūmnā).
adj. Shaking, swaying, waving; moving (the head) up and down (as quadrupeds).
s.f. = jhumari, q.v. s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode (cf. jhūmar, and jhūmnā).
s. Anger, rage.
s.m. A bunch or cluster (of flowers or fruit); a tuft, a tassel; the bell-shaped pendant (of an ear-ring); the passion-flower; the flower Gloriosa superba; Charles's Wain, Ursa Major; the Pleiades
more: n. m. 1. A cluster; a bunch of flowers or fruit. 2. The bell-shaped pendant of an earring. 3. ( sāt sahelyoṅ kā jhūmkā ) Charles's wain; Ursa major. 4. A flower; Gloriosa superba .
s. m. 1. The bell-shaped pendant of an ear-ring. 2. The wain, Ursa major. 3. A bunch of flowers or fruit. 4. Name of a flower.

def: s.m. = H جهومکي झूमकी jhūmkī, s.f. = jhumkā, jhumkī, qq.v.
more: s. m. 1. The bell-shaped pendant of an ear-ring. 2. The constellation of the bear. See جهمکا jhumkā.

def: s.f. A kind of ear-ring with a pendant.
s.f. = H جهومکا झूमका jhūmkā, s.m. = jhumkā, jhumkī, qq.v.
more: s. f. A kind of ear-ring. See جهمکا jhumkā.

ety: prob. fr. S. क्षुभ्; cf. jhūlnā
def: v.n. To shake, to sway to and fro or from side to side, to wave; to stagger, roll, reel; to move with stately steps, to strut; to move (the head) up and down (as an animal); to nod (through sleep); to hang loosely, hang down; to droop; to gather, to lower (as clouds).
more: v. n. 1. To hang down; droop; nod; reel. 1. Shām se hī jhūmne lagā . 2. Jhūmte jhāmte shām ko pauṅche . Reeling from side to side he at length reached home by evening. 2. To gather; lower (clouds). Abr jhūm rahā hai . 3. To move with measured steps or with dignity; to strut; come sailing along. jhūm jhūm , adj. 1. Gathering or lowering of the clouds. Jhūm jhūm-kar barastā hai . 2. Drooping or closing of the eyes. Saīyāṅ, jhūm jhūm nindyā āī re . Song. My love, my eyes with sleep hang down and droop!
v. n. To wave, to move the head up and down, to slumber, to move loose, to gather (the clouds).

def: s.f. (dialec.), Name of a Hindū festival.
s.f. A slight resemblance.

ety: S. जूर्ण, rt. जुर्, or जीर्ण, rt. ज्ऋ+कं
def: s.m. A ripe cocoa-nut; — a kind of muslin (syn. malmal).
more: n. m. H. jhinjhinā thin. A kind of thin muslin.
s. m. 1. A ripe cocoa-nut. 2. A kind of muslin.

ety: Prk.-S. झुण्टः, prob. fr. S. जुटः or जूटः
def: s.m. A shrub, bush; underwood; a tuft (of grass, &c.); a clump (of trees); a thicket; thick hair, mop (of hair); a ragged-looking tree.
more: s. m. 1. A crowd, swarm, flock, troop; the buzzing noise of a crowd. 2. A clump (of trees).

ety: Prk. जुत्थं; S. यूथं; but cf. the preceding
def: s.m. A multitude, crowd, troop; a flock, herd; a swarm; — the buzzing noise of a crowd
s.m. A heap; a crowd; an association (see jhuṅḍ).

n. m. S. झुराट 1. Underbush; a tuft of grass; a thicket. 2. A swarm; flock; troop; crowd. 3. A thick head of hair. 4. A ragged-looking tree. jhunḍ ke jhunḍ . Dense crowds. jhuṅ'ḍī , n. f. The roots of grain stalks, sugarcane, etc.

ety: jhunḍ, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A bush; a tuft (of grass); thicket, jungle; the root of grain stalks, sugar-cane, &c.
more: s. f. A bush, a tuft of grass.

def: s.f. A lot or parcel of land in a coparcenary village; the amount due from each sharer in a coparcenary estate; — a clump of grass, &c. ( = jhunḍī, q.v.).
more: n. f. A clump of grass.
